title: Meeting Rooms II
problem_statement: Given meeting time intervals, find the minimum number of conference rooms required.

examples.explanation: Two rooms are needed because meetings [0,30] and [5,10] overlap.

intuition: Track ongoing meetings with a min-heap of end times and reuse rooms when a meeting finishes.

approaches.name: Min-heap of end times
approaches.description: Sort meetings by start time, then use a min-heap to store end times and free rooms as meetings end.
approaches.why_not: Counting overlaps without a heap is error-prone and can miss room reuse opportunities.

interview_qa.a: The heap stores end times for meetings currently using rooms.

interview_qa.q: When can a room be reused?
interview_qa.a: When the next meeting starts at or after the earliest end time in the heap.
